---
title: "S0: Opposition"
---

S0 is the two-point opposition layer. It is included as a small finite base for sign and antipodal language.

::: {.learning-unit}

## What To Learn Here

S0 is the smallest possible opposition system: two points and a flip between them. It prepares the vocabulary later reused for sign, antipodes, poles, and paired states.

:::

## Lesson Path

1. Read [Opposition](opposition.qmd).
2. Check how a two-point flip differs from a larger circular rotation.
3. Use the theorem and dictionary cards as the source trail.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why is S0 useful even though it has only two points?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S0-T0001"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="S0-0001"></div>

See [Opposition](opposition.qmd).

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
